title: Non-standard characters should not occur in header file names in #include directives
description: Non-standard characters should not occur in header file names in "#include" directivesAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEbased-on-misraWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe#includedirective allows you to include header files in your code by enclosing the file names in either double quotes (") or angle brackets (<and>). However, using:',\,//or/*characters between the double quotes (for example:#include "dir\foo.h")',\,",//or/*characters between the angle brackets (for example:#include <"foo">)in the header names may or may not be supported, and the behavior will depend on the specific implementation.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLa
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: #include "file//header.h" // Noncompliant: double slash in header name
compliant: #include "dir/foo.h" // Compliant: forward slash for directory separator
#include <stdio.h> // Compliant: standard header with no special characters
#include "myheader.h" // Compliant: simple filename with no special characters
#include <sys/types.h> // Compliant: standard path with forward slash

checks.message: Non-standard characters (backslashes, double slashes, quotes in angle brackets, special characters) should not occur in header file names. Use forward slashes for paths and avoid special characters in #include directives.
